title: Flash function missing nonReentrant modifier allows reentrancy via flashCallback
claim: The flash() function declares modifiers `external transactable noDelegateCall isNotEmergency` but omits `nonReentrant`. Every other state-changing function in Curve.sol (originSwap, targetSwap, deposit, depositWithWhitelist, withdraw, emergencyWithdraw, transfer, transferFrom, approve) carries the nonReentrant modifier. During flash()'s IFlashCallback(msg.sender).flashCallback() external call (line 656), the reentrancy guard `notEntered` remains true, allowing the callback contract to call deposit(), originSwap(), targetSwap(), or withdraw() — all of which check `notEntered` and will pass because flash() never set it to false.
impact: An attacker can flash-borrow pool tokens and during flashCallback call deposit() to mint LP tokens at a manipulated pool ratio (the borrowed tokens are temporarily absent, so _oGLiq is artificially low, inflating the LP token count). After returning the borrowed tokens (or having deposit() consume them) and satisfying the balance checks, the attacker holds more LP tokens than their fair share. They then call withdraw() to redeem at the restored normal ratio, extracting the difference as profit. The attack requires the pool to be unfrozen (frozen=false — currently frozen=true on-chain), which is the only barrier. The owner can unfreeze at any time, making this a latent but concrete vulnerability.
paths: 1. Deploy attacker contract implementing IFlashCallback with a flashCallback() that calls Curve.deposit(attackerCapital, deadline)
2. When pool is unfrozen, call Curve.flash(attackerContract, borrowAmount, 0, data)
3. flash() transfers borrowAmount of token0 to attackerContract; notEntered stays true
4. flashCallback() executes: calls Curve.deposit() which computes _oGLiq based on diminished token0 balance, yielding inflated LP tokens
5. deposit() pulls tokens from attackerContract (including the borrowed tokens plus attacker's own capital), satisfying the later balance check
6. flashCallback() returns; balance0After >= balance0Before + fee0 passes because deposit() already returned tokens to the pool
7. Attacker calls Curve.withdraw() to burn inflated LP tokens and receive more assets than deposited
8. Profit = value withdrawn minus value deposited
